text:
It's the first day (officially at least) of the greatest show in sport and Irish competitors are in action across ten different disciplines at Paris 2024.

Eventing rider Sarah Ennis is up first at 8.48am in the dressage phase of that three-part equestrian format while world champion gymnast Rhys McClenaghan will close the day by getting back on the pommel horse some time after 8.30pm.

Ryan Mullen is the only Irish competitor who could finish the day on the podium: he's out in the men's cycling time trial just after 3.30pm.

You can follow the Olympic Games action all day with our live tracker on RTÉ Sport Online and the RTÉ News app, watch live from 9.15am-10.30pm on RTÉ2 and RTÉ Player, and listen to updates on RTÉ Radio 1 and 2fm.

(in order of start time)

0830-1730Eventing, team and individual dressage:0848 Sarah Ennis(Action Lady M),1142 Susie Berry(Wellfields Lincoln),1551 Austin O'Connor(Colorado Blue)

0930-1100Men’s Pool B: Belgium vIreland(Round 1)1215-1345 Men's Pool B: Australia v Argentina (Round 1)

1000-1012Women’s 100m butterfly heats:Ellen Walshe(Heat 2)

1115-1126Women’s 4x100m freestyle relay heats:Danielle Hill, Victoria Catterson, Erin O’RiordanandGrace Davidson(Heat 1)

1930-1942Women's 100m butterfly semi-finals

2034-2044Women's 4x100m freestyle relay final

1050-1100Men's double sculls:Philip Doyle, Daire Lynch(Heat 3)

1110-1120Women’s double sculls:Zoe Hyde, Alison Bergin(Heat 2)

1622Men’s 63.5kg (lightweight):Dean Clancyv Obada Alkasbeh (Jordan)

1130-1500Women's time trial

1534-1701Men’s time trial:Ryan Mullen

1400-1452, 1610-1702Men's C1 heats:Liam Jegou

1500-1602, 1710-1812Women’s K1 heats:Madison Corcoran

1400Men’s 5th-8th place play-off semi-final:Irelandv USA1430 Men's semi-final: South Africa v France1500 Men's semi-final: Fiji v Australia1700 Men's 7th/8th place play-off1730 Men's 5th/6th place play-off1800 Men's bronze medal match1845 Men's final

2010Men's Singles – Group P:Nhat Nguyenv Misha Zilberman (Israel)

2040-2105 approxMen's pommel horse qualifying:Rhys McClenaghan

That is the extent of the Irish involvement on Saturday but there will also be medals handed out in men's and women's 400m freestyle swimming, women's 3m synchronised diving, fencing, judo, skateboarding and shooting.

The tennis events also begin on the clay courts of Roland Garros, with women's world no 1 Iga Swiatek first on at Court Philippe-Chatrier against Romania's Irina-Camelia Begu at 11am, followed by 24-time Grand Slam title winner Novak Djokovic against Australian Matthew Ebden.

Watch the 2024 Olympic Games with 14 hours of televised action on RTÉ2 and RTÉ Player each day. Listen to extensive radio coverage on RTÉ Radio 1 and 2fm's Game On and follow each moment from Paris on RTÉ.ie, the RTÉ News app and all RTÉ digital platforms. Listen to the daily RTÉ Sport Olympics Podcast.
